text.title: Structural Design Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the structural-patterns methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Pattern selection MUST start from a concrete signal: incompatible interfaces → Adapter; class explosion across orthogonal axes → Bridge; tree-of-uniform-leaves → Composite; behaviour stacking on one type → Decorator; broad subsystem entry-point → Facade; access control / lazy load → Proxy; many identical immutable instances → Flyweight.

rationale.source: Gang of Four; Refactoring to Patterns (Kerievsky).

rule.rationale: Pattern-without-signal produces speculative indirection; signal-first selection matches problem to mechanism.

rule.statement: Each candidate pattern MUST carry an indirection cost (extra classes / files / runtime hops) and the cost MUST be weighed against documented symptom severity.
rule.rationale: Structural patterns add abstraction; uncosted picks produce towers of indirection that obscure intent.

rule.statement: Decorator stack depth MUST NOT exceed 2 per call site. Cross-cutting stacks (logging + auth + retry + cache + tracing) MUST be composed via middleware pipelines, NOT nested decorators.
rule.rationale: Deep decorator stacks make stack traces unreadable and resolution of cross-cutting policy impossible; middleware composition is the documented alternative.

rule.statement: Facade pattern MUST be applied to a coherent subsystem with a small, stable public surface. A 'Facade' aggregating unrelated domains is a god-class antipattern and MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Facades that grow without scope become god-classes — the very smell they were meant to dissolve.

rule.statement: Flyweight MUST be applied only when memory profiling shows the duplicated objects dominate the heap (≥10% of resident set or > 100MB). Speculative Flyweight produces complexity for no gain.
rule.rationale: Flyweight is the most invasive structural pattern; without measured pressure it pays no dividend.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (signal, catalog, test coverage, impact estimate), apply r1 → r5 and emit the ADR.
rule.rationale: Order encodes risk: signal first, cost second, decorator cap third, facade-scope fourth, flyweight evidence fifth.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) no concrete signal, (b) trivial CRUD, (c) symptom belongs to behavioural / creational family. Exit with note.
rule.rationale: Wrong-family or signal-less application yields negative-value indirection.
